title: ficus subacuminata
intro: Ficus subacuminata, also known as the Taiwan banyan or Taiwan strangler fig, is a species of fig tree native to Taiwan and southern China. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ficus subacuminata include reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and treating various skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Ficus subacuminata include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are typically made from the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant.
